ground_truth.title: Scenic Turkish and French sights with lowjhg
ground_truth.author: lowjhg
ground_truth.main_content:
lowjhg
Feb 14, 2026 | 9:05 pm

Im writing this Trip report, inspired by JapesUK's live trip report.

Now, this is my first trip report, less alone a live trip report, so bear with me.

Many of us will know me as the 'guy who runs SIN DO', and nothing apart from that.

In this journey, I'm bringing all of you to see a different side of me!

Gird your loins, our trip will unfold shortly.

lowjhg
Feb 14, 2026 | 9:07 pm

Contents

Chapter 1: Pre-trip Spiel
Chapter 2: Trip Facts
Chapter 3: Pre-departure musings
Chapter 4: The positioning flight
Chapter 5: ...

and so on...

lowjhg
Feb 14, 2026 | 9:08 pm

Chapter 1: Pre-trip Spiel

The activator of this trip started out with finding a relatively reasonable fare on Emirates for travel in First from Asia to Europe. This will be my 4th roundtrip in this current Skywards membership year. I have a further trip booked before the end of my membership year to bring me to Platinum.

I knew I wanted to explore Turkey for a long time but hadn't had the opportunity to do so, so when a wine and shopping trip idea came up, I knew I had to add Turkey into the mix. Thankfully, the EK airfare did not change too much.

I'd be flying from Jakarta (CGK) through to Dubai (DXB) then into Istanbul (IST), and back from Paris (CDG) back through to Dubai (DXB) to Jakarta (CGK). Being based in Singapore (SIN), I needed to position to Jakarta (CGK) and SQ redemptions it was. Ex Singapore (SIN) will feature Singapore Airlines (SQ) Business and back will feature Singapore Airlines (SQ) First. In between Turkey and France, I've booked a few Turkish (TK) and an Air France (AF) sectors to give variety to the journey.

This report will feature a lot of Hyatt properties with a lot of Hyatt fawning. You are warned.

Also, Hotels and trips planning made possible by Holiday.with.Ken
Excluding booking flights and restaurants, that's my speciality.

So sit back, relax and come with me on my magical adventure!

lowjhg
Feb 14, 2026 | 9:37 pm

Chapter 2: Trip Facts

Our journey today takes form of
SIN-CGK-DXB-IST-ASR-IST-BOD-CDG-DXB-CGK-SIN.

A journey of 16991 miles, according to gcmap.

This journey features the following airlines (listed in alphabetical order):

Air France (AF)
Emirates (EK)
Singapore Airlines (SQ)
Turkish Airlines (TK)

This journey features the following hotels (listed in alphabetical order):

Argos in Cappadocia
FirstName Bordeaux
Hotel du Louvre
Park Hyatt Istanbul - Macka Palas
Park Hyatt Jakarta

This journey features the following restaurants (listed in chronological order):

Foxy Nisantasi - Istanbul
Ali Ocakbasi Karakoy - Istanbul
Efendy istanbul - Istanbul
Seki Restaurant and Bar - Cappadocia
Amicis - Bordeaux
Quanjude - Bordeaux
Helen Restaurant - Paris
La Tour d'Argent - Paris

Holiday.with.Ken
Feb 14, 2026 | 10:24 pm

Very exciting! Looking forward to it.

WiSK
Feb 15, 2026 | 12:41 am

That's a very intriguing routing!

lowjhg
Feb 16, 2026 | 9:37 pm

Chapter 3: Pre-departure musings

It's exactly 3 hours before we're supposed to head to the airport. Suitcase count: 7. Three small and 4 large.

Today's journey will take us to Jakarta, where we position to pick up the wonderfully cheap Emirates First fare. We are departing on SQ964 at 5:20pm later this afternoon.

Just woke up after an all-nighter of festivities to welcome in the Year of the Horse. currently having a double macchiato as we speak.

Lunch is in an hour. I'm more than excited.

Can't this trip start already?

lowjhg
Feb 17, 2026 | 9:16 am

Chapter 4: The positioning flight

3pm quickly rolled by. It was time to get changed and begin the journey to Istanbul.

Singapore was very rainy when I left home for the airport, but the rain did not relent upon arrival. The Grab fare costed S$74, very expensive by Singapore standards. Unfortunately we're held hostage by the policies which require booking a Grab 6 when you have more than 2 luggages.

Singapore Changi Terminal 2 was hustling and bustling (shoutout to Jayden ie oneworldflyer) when we got there. Thankfully the Singapore Airlines Business Class check in counter was queue-less. Our boarding passes were issues and baggage tagged to Jakarta. Off to Immigration.

Terminal 2 is an interesting place for me. As a oneworld-centric traveller, im more used to Terminal 1 where the multitudes of OW lounges exist, or Terminal 4 for the Cathay Pacific lounge. At SIN, all star alliance partner airlines use SQ's lounges. Today was no different. Their terminal 2 Silverkris Lounge, Business side is a zoo, as usual. We only had 40 minutes to chow before heading to the boarding gate.

But first, a mandatory lo-hei to celebrate the new year!

As we got to the gate, boarding was already in its final stages. Today's chariot is 9V-SNC, a 10 years old Boeing 777-300ER. This is a new frame for me.

Flight No: SQ964
Date: 17th February 2026
ETD: 5:20pm (GMT+8)
ATD: 5:47pm (GMT+8)
ETA: 6:05pm (GMT+7)
ATA: 6:41pm (GMT+7)
Flight time: 1h 54m

A few pictures of the seat.

Some Champagne on boarding.

Moments later, departure time came, and went. Pilot came on the intercom and explained an hours delay and attributed it to connecting pax and missed takeoff slots. I was already thirsty by then and could only make do with the bottle of water on the seat.

Today's meal is from SQ's Book the Cook service, a Ayam Percik dan Nasi Ulam. Spicy, with a kick, but delicious.

The rest of the flight went over uneventfully. Arrived at gate 6, a short walk to immigration, thankfully. Not the usual Gate 1 nonsense. By the time we cleared immigration, bags were already out. Straight through customs and back to the departure hall.

To be continued...

for shorter trips, thats my whole 2 nightss worth of clothes and wares.

lowjhg
Feb 19, 2026 | 11:38 pm

Going on a tour today of Istanbul. Also looking forward to Cappadocia tomorrow!

lowjhg
Feb 19, 2026 | 11:47 pm

Chapter 5: Welcome back to CGK

So, SQ has an interline agreement with Emirates. This means theoretically I could check my bags through Jakarta and do an international to international transit at CGK.

No, we didnt do that. We went the old fashioned way, grabbed luggage, cleared immigration and customs, went back up to the departures to check in again. I just dont trust the local handler to get the baggage onto the new flight, even though we had 6 hours in between flights.

Emirates check in for flight EK359 opened at 9pm sharp. We had been in the departures area, sitting at a bench for nearly 2 hours. My fault. I miscalculated the check in opening time.

So we got a bottle of water, and people watched. TK check in opened and closed, same for MU. Next is our turn. Fortunately there were only 5 passengers in F today. The queue was empty. Interesting to see that this route does not attract a lot of platinums.

To be continued
